# Counterfactual Designs for Manufactured Traction

To prove a tactic works, ask: what would have happened without the manufactured signal?

## 1. Randomized exposure test
Randomly expose users to manipulated vs unmanipulated social proof. Measure clicks, likes, shares, purchases, or ratings.

Best for: fake reviews, visible likes, social proof widgets, badges.

## 2. Matched synthetic control
Compare a campaign item to matched items with similar baseline quality, follower count, category, release time, and prior growth.

Best for: clipping campaigns, trend simulation, launch platforms.

## 3. Interrupted time series
Observe a metric before and after a suspected manipulation window or enforcement removal.

Best for: bot removal, platform demotion, fake-review purge, app-store enforcement.

## 4. Difference-in-differences
Compare treated and untreated groups before and after an intervention or exposure.

Best for: search spam updates, review-warning labels, disclosure changes.

## 5. Network null model
Keep account activity levels fixed, shuffle timestamps or targets, and compare observed coordination edges to simulated distribution.

Best for: coordinated posting, link sharing, comment seeding.

## 6. Holdout lift test
Withhold paid/incentivized distribution from a subset of comparable audiences or geographies.

Best for: ads, influencer campaigns, affiliate traffic.

## Warning
Most public investigations cannot run all tests. The public proof standard is usually convergent evidence, not perfect causal identification.
